page.title: Hachiman Shrine (Mugiya, Yuuki-cho, Saeki-ku, Hiroshima City)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八幡神社 (広島市佐伯区湯来町麦谷)]]
{{Infobox religious building |名称= 八幡神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = {{画像募集中|cat=広島市}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location= {{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|qid=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima|1=Hiroshima}}[[佐伯区]]湯来町大字麦谷1451 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.6 | 経度度 = 132 | 経度分 = 18 | 経度秒 =0.6 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]]、{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}、[[:en:Emperor Chuai|Emperor Chuai]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type= |創建= |本殿= 三間社[[流造]]、桟瓦葺 |別名= 麦谷八幡神社 |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= [[10月]]第3[[日曜日]] |神事= |location = {{ill|Saeki-ku|qid=Q73718|1=Saeki-ku, Hiroshima}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.505722|N|132.300167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Hachiman Shrine''' (八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|en|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|qid=Q73718|lt=Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|1=Saeki-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|qid=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima City|1=Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. It is also known as Mugidani Hachiman Shrine (麦谷八幡神社).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (品陀和気命, Hondawake-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} (帯中津日子命, Tarashinakatsuhiko-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (息長帯日売命, Okinagatarashihime-no-mikoto)
== History ==
* According to ancient documents stored at the shrine, during the {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|qid=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun era|1=Tenbun}} (1532–1555), villagers from the five villages of Minouchi sided with the forces of {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|qid=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} in his conflict with {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|qid=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}}. After Mōri’s victory, they received a golden ritual staff (金幣) from {{ill|Yoshida Hachiman Shrine|ja|吉田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504524|1=Yoshida Hachiman Shrine}} in Takata District. When they placed it at Hitomichihara in Minokusa, Mugidani Village, mysterious lights appeared each night. Fearing divine intervention, the villagers of Tada, Sugazawa, Wada, Mugidani, and Shimo gathered and decided to construct a shrine at Minokusa. This shrine was consecrated in the ninth year of Tenbun (1540). Later, the ritual staff was divided among the five villages, each establishing its own shrine.
* The {{ill|Geihan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geihan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11615959}} records that in September 1540 (Tenbun 10), Kazusa Kurōbyōe Tadaaki (上総九郎兵衛忠秋), the lord of {{ill|Kashiwabara Castle (Hiroshima)|ja|栢原城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwabara Castle (Hiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a vassal of the Mōri clan, transferred a golden ritual staff from Yoshida Hachiman Shrine in Takata District to this shrine. Later, individuals named Wada Tōzaemon and Kido Jūrōemon also dedicated a divine mirror (神鏡) from Yoshida Hachiman Shrine to this shrine. Two sets of old armor stored at the shrine were said to have belonged to Wada and Kido.
* {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|qid=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}} granted sacred lands (神田) to the shrine, but these were later lost.
* In 1669 (Kanbun 9), Oda Gorōzaemon rebuilt the shrine.
== Architecture ==
* The main hall (本殿) is a three-bay shrine structure (三間社) in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} architectural style with tiled roofing (桟瓦葺).
== Annual Festival ==
* Held on the third {{ill|Sunday|en|Sunday|qid=Q132|lt=Sunday|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}}.
== References ==
